reason: Both answers correctly land on Postgres-backed queue, cite FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, transactional enqueue as the key differentiator, list concrete off-the-shelf libraries instead of hand-rolling, and give sensible thresholds for revisiting the decision. B slightly edges out A by explicitly connecting SQS advantages to fan-out/multi-service scenarios, giving more concrete failure-mode detail for the alternatives (Redis job loss without persistence config, SQS visibility timeout quirks), and adding a useful burst-tolerance caveat. A is also correct and solid but its treatment of when the alternatives make sense is thinner. Neither has factual errors; both are similarly concise.
